property.name: Club Hotel Dolphin

property.address: Kammala South, Waikkal, Negombo 61150, Western Province, Sri Lanka

items.name: Babysitting Service

items.name: Luggage Storage
items.description: Secure storage before check-in or after check-out.

items.name: Eggs Your Way

items.description: Special Sri Lankan curry buffet every Thursday evening.

items.name: Seafood BBQ (Saturdays)
items.description: Fresh catch BBQ on the beach terrace every Saturday night.

restaurants.name: The Lagoon — À La Carte
restaurants.description: A premium à la carte restaurant by the lagoon for special evenings. Advance reservations required.

items.name: Negombo Crab Curry
items.description: Famous Negombo blue crab in a roasted coconut gravy.

items.name: Grilled Sea Bass
items.description: Whole sea bass, lemon caper butter, roasted vegetables.

items.name: Prawn & Mango Salad
items.description: Tiger prawns, green mango, coconut dressing.

bars.name: Splash Pool Bar
bars.description: Swim-up bar at the main pool. All-inclusive cocktails, mocktails, and cold beers throughout the day.

treatments.name: Relaxation Massage
treatments.description: A soothing full-body massage to unwind after a day in the sun.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 2 hours in advance.

treatments.name: Hot Stone Therapy
treatments.description: Heated basalt stones placed along the body's energy centres for deep relaxation.
treatments.cancellationPolicy: Cancel at least 2 hours in advance.

classes.name: Morning Stretch & Tone
classes.description: A gentle stretch class by the pool. Perfect for all ages and fitness levels.

options.name: Airport Arrival Transfer
options.description: Quick 20-minute transfer from BIA to Club Hotel Dolphin.

options.name: Airport Departure Transfer
options.description: Transfer to BIA — allow 1.5 hours before flight.

experiences.name: Negombo Lagoon Fishing Trip
experiences.description: Join local fishermen on a traditional outrigger canoe for an early morning fishing trip across the lagoon. Whatever you catch gets cooked for your breakfast!

experiences.name: Dolphin Watching Catamaran
experiences.description: Head out to sea on a catamaran in search of spinner dolphins and flying fish. The ocean just beyond the lagoon teems with marine life.

localPlaces.name: Negombo Fish Market (Lellama)
localPlaces.description: One of Sri Lanka's most vibrant and atmospheric working fish markets, where outrigger fishing canoes unload their overnight catch of tuna, Spanish mackerel, snapper, squid, and ray at the Lellama landing site. The scale of activity — ice-packers, buyers, auctioneers, and gull-chasing cats — makes it one of the most photogenic scenes on the west coast. The market is predominantly run by Negombo's large Catholic fishing community.
localPlaces.openingHours: Daily 03:30–12:00 (peak action 05:30–08:00; closed most of Sunday)
localPlaces.hotelNote: The very best time is 05:30–07:00 when the night boats return. Our front desk arranges tuk-tuks the evening before at a fixed rate. Bring a camera with good low-light capability.

localPlaces.name: Muthurajawela Wetlands & Dutch Canal
localPlaces.description: Sri Lanka's largest coastal peat bog and a nationally protected wetland, covering 3,068 hectares between the Negombo Lagoon and Colombo. Boat safaris glide along the historic 17th-century Dutch Canal through mangrove tunnels, past egret colonies and purple herons, with regular sightings of water monitors, crocodiles, and over 60 bird species. The Dutch built the canal in the 1650s to link Negombo to Colombo as a spice-trade artery.
localPlaces.openingHours: Visitor Centre daily 07:00–18:00; boat tours available all day
localPlaces.entryFee: Approx. USD 20–25 per person (includes boat and guide); park entry extra
localPlaces.hotelNote: The early morning safari (07:00) is best for birdwatching. The evening tour catches the light beautifully. Our concierge pre-books slots with Sarath Boat Tours — one of the most reputable operators on the canal.

localPlaces.name: Negombo Old Town & St Mary's Church
localPlaces.description: Negombo's Portuguese and Dutch colonial heritage is visible in its fort ruins, canal-side architecture, and an extraordinary density of Catholic churches — earning it the nickname 'Little Rome'. St Mary's Church on Main Street is the largest church in Sri Lanka, famous for its thunderous painted ceiling murals depicting the life of the Virgin Mary; it draws pilgrims from across the island. The town's lagoon-side promenade is a pleasant evening stroll.
localPlaces.openingHours: St Mary's Church open daily (mass times vary); town accessible always
localPlaces.hotelNote: The Dutch Fort remnants (now a prison) and the old rest house are just 5 minutes' walk from the church. Combine with the fish market for a half-day Negombo town excursion.

localPlaces.name: Negombo Lagoon
localPlaces.description: A 3,000-hectare saltwater lagoon separating the Negombo peninsula from the mainland — one of Sri Lanka's most productive fishing grounds and a stunning natural backdrop. At dawn, hundreds of traditional outrigger canoes (oruwa) set off in convoy for the open sea; at dusk they return in silhouette against a fiery sky. Catamaran and kayak tours of the lagoon can be arranged from the beach near the hotel.
localPlaces.openingHours: Best viewed at sunrise and sunset
localPlaces.entryFee: Free to view; boat tours from LKR 2,000/hour
localPlaces.hotelNote: Our guest-favourite experience is a sunrise catamaran trip on the lagoon (departs 05:45) — the returning fishing fleet creates a spectacular sight. Book through our concierge.

localPlaces.name: Bandaranaike International Airport (BIA)
localPlaces.description: Sri Lanka's primary international airport, situated just 19 km from the hotel — one of the most convenient proximity relationships of any resort on the island. Guests can arrive from a long-haul flight and be poolside within 25 minutes, making Club Hotel Dolphin the natural first and last night of any Sri Lanka itinerary.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Our airport transfer service (LKR 3,500 one-way) operates 24/7. For early-morning departures we offer a complimentary packed breakfast and express checkout the night before.

localPlaces.name: Pamunugama Beach
localPlaces.description: A quieter, less developed stretch of golden sand beach north of Waikkal, popular with local families and largely undiscovered by international tourists. Traditional fishing boats line the shore; palm trees frame the horizon. The beach is excellent for long walks, shell collecting, and watching traditional stilt fishermen at work in the shallow waters.
localPlaces.hotelNote: Best for an early morning or late afternoon walk — midday sun is intense. Combine with a visit to the Pamunugama church and fishing village for an authentic local experience.
